title: senecio jacobaea var. borysthenicus
intro: Senecio jacobaea var. borysthenicus, also known as the Tansy Ragwort, is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in their ability to inhibit the growth of cancer cells and exhibit anti-inflammatory effects. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, with its health benefits attributed to its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. Senecio jacobaea var. borysthenicus is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity, and it is not recommended for long-term consumption.
